message:
refactor(sessions): defer auth checks and improve render flow

Restructure the sessions list page to improve code organization and
avoid unnecessary early returns that complicate the component flow.

## Key Changes

### 1. Deferred Lab Mode Check
Before: Early return with LockedState if lab mode disabled
After: Store lock reason in variable, render LockedState in main JSX

This allows the component to have a single return statement and makes
the render logic more explicit and easier to follow.

### 2. Conditional Auth Check
Before: Always called checkOrchestrationAuth() then early returned
After: Only call checkOrchestrationAuth() when lab mode is enabled

```typescript
const pageAuth = labModeEnabled ? checkOrchestrationAuth(...) : null;
```

This avoids unnecessary auth processing when lab mode is disabled.

### 3. Conditional Data Fetching
Before: Always attempted to fetch threads, caught errors
After: Only fetch threads when not locked (`if (!labLockedReason) { ... }`)

This prevents unnecessary AgentMailClient initialization and API calls
when the page will show a locked state anyway.

### 4. Unified Render Logic
Before: Multiple early returns scattered throughout
After: Single return with conditional rendering

```tsx
{labLockedReason ? (
  <LockedState reason={labLockedReason} />
) : !loadError && threads.length === 0 ? (
  <EmptyState />
) : ( ... )}
```

## Benefits

- Cleaner control flow with single return statement
- Reduced unnecessary work when page is locked
- Easier to reason about component state
- Consistent with React best practices for conditional rendering
